raw_text:
Goal: To investigate the effect of laser power on porosity in a DED NiTi process.

raw_text:
Goal: To investigate the effect of powder feed rate on build height in a Direct Energy Deposition (DED) NiTi process.
Hypothesis: Increasing powder feed rate will result in an increase in build height.
Resources Used:
- DED machine
- SEM
- XRD
- precision saw
- mounting press
- grinding and polishing kit
Independent Variables:
- Powder feed rate
Dependent Variable:
- Build height
Controls:
- All non-target process settings fixed
Conditions:
- Explicit numeric powder feed rate levels
Replicates:
- Three independent runs
Procedure Outline:
1. Set up DED NiTi process with specified powder feed rate levels.
2. Prepare SEM cross-sections for measurement.
3. Measure build height for each run.
4. Compare build height responses across different feed rates.
5. Analyze data for significant differences between feed rates.
Analysis Plan:
1. Use ANOVA to determine if there is a significant difference in build height between feed rates.
2. Interpret results to determine if increasing powder feed rate leads to an increase in build height

[baseline_safe_candidate]

reasoning_trace.resource_check: DED machine; SEM; precision saw; mounting press; grinding and polishing kit; NiTi powder; argon gas; mounting resin
reasoning_trace.control_strategy: Keep all non-powder feed rate settings fixed across all conditions.; Use the same NiTi powder lot, argon shielding setup, and sample geometry for every run.; Prepare every sample with the same sectioning, mounting, grinding, and polishing workflow before SEM imaging.
reasoning_trace.measurement_strategy: Quantify build height from SEM cross-sections for every replicate using one consistent image-analysis workflow.; Record the exact numeric powder feed rate level and replicate identifier for every sample.; Summarize build height by condition before comparing the low, midpoint, and high settings.
reasoning_trace.risk_check: Feasible contrast variant that widens the numeric screening span while trading off some replication.

final_proposal.goal: Design one feasible experiment to study how powder feed rate affects build height in a DED NiTi process.
final_proposal.hypothesis: Within the previously validated operating window, changing powder feed rate will change build height.
controls: Keep all non-powder feed rate settings fixed across all conditions.
Use the same NiTi powder lot, argon shielding setup, and sample geometry for every run.

conditions: Powder feed rate = 85% of the validated baseline value
Powder feed rate = 95% of the validated baseline value
Powder feed rate = 105% of the validated baseline value
Powder feed rate = 115% of the validated baseline value
procedure_outline: Run one DED build at each explicit numeric powder feed rate condition within the previously validated stable operating window.
Keep the same powder lot, shielding setup, geometry, and all non-target process settings across conditions.
Section each build with the precision saw, then mount, grind, and polish for cross-section imaging.
Acquire SEM cross-sections for every replicate and record the porosity measurement for each sample.
Use XRD only as a secondary sanity check if phase consistency needs confirmation.

measurement_plan: Quantify build height from SEM cross-sections for every replicate using one consistent image-analysis workflow.
Record the exact numeric powder feed rate level and replicate identifier for every sample.
Summarize build height by condition before comparing the low, midpoint, and high settings.
analysis_plan: Screen mean build height across the wider numeric powder feed rate range.
Use the result to choose a narrower follow-up window.
feasibility_checks: Uses only listed equipment and materials.
Expands the numeric powder feed rate span while keeping the plan executable as a first-pass screen.
Retains evidence alignment and core controls.

raw_text:
Goal: To investigate the effect of laser power on crack density in a Direct Energy Deposition (DED) NiTi process.
Hypothesis: Increasing laser power will result in increased crack density.
Resources Used:
- DED machine
- SEM
- XRD
- precision saw
- mounting press
- grinding and polishing kit
Independent Variables:
- Laser power (numeric factor level)
Dependent Variable:
- Crack density (numeric measurement)
Controls:
- All non-target process settings (fixed)
Conditions:
- Explicit numeric laser power levels
Replicates:
- Multiple samples taken from the same build
Procedure Outline:
1. Set up DED machine with specified laser power levels.
2. Mount and polish sample parts.
3. Take SEM cross-sections of the mounted and polished samples.
4. Measure crack density using XRD.
5. Repeat steps 2-4 for multiple samples.
6. Analyze data to determine relationship between laser power and crack density.
Measurement Plan:
- SEM cross-sections of mounted and polished samples
- XRD analysis of cracked areas
-
